notable_external_news: MAJOR story not confirmable from monitored sources — On June 24, Anthropic publicly accused Alibaba of the largest known AI distillation attack ever: ~25,000 fraudulent accounts ran 28.8M Claude API exchanges (Apr 22–Jun 5) to harvest model capabilities for Alibaba's Qwen AI lab. Anthropic formally notified the US Senate Banking Committee; Alibaba stock fell ~3%. Covered by Bloomberg, CNBC, Tom's Hardware, The Next Web, TweakTown, and TechTimes. Manually check XDA and The Verge for their take — this story likely appeared on both and would be high-value social content.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.191 — /rewind command, MCP reliability improvements, 37% CPU reduction
updates.summary: Anthropic released Claude Code v2.1.191 on June 24, introducing /rewind — a command that restores the conversation to the state it was in before /clear was run, recovering lost session context instantly. The update also cuts streaming CPU usage by ~37% by coalescing text updates to 100ms intervals and improves MCP server reliability with automatic retries on transient network errors. Over 20 bugs were fixed, including background agents resurrecting after being stopped, scroll-position jumping during active streaming, and /voice showing a generic error when disabled by org policy.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the official Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog. Version number, date, and every feature detail verified from official release notes. No red flags.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.193 — Shell auto-classification, live bash autocomplete, OpenTelemetry assistant logging
updates.summary: Anthropic shipped Claude Code v2.1.193 on June 25, adding autoMode.classifyAllShell — a setting that routes every Bash and PowerShell command through Claude's security classifier rather than only high-risk patterns, giving teams tighter control over AI-generated shell actions. Developers also get live file path autocomplete in bash mode (!) and a new OpenTelemetry log event capturing assistant responses for enterprise audit pipelines. Multiple background agent lifecycle fixes and improved MCP OAuth with automatic header-refresh on 401/403 errors are also included.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the official Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog. Version number, date, and all feature details verified from official release notes. OpenTelemetry note: the new OTEL_LOG_ASSISTANT_RESPONSES var defaults to follow OTEL_LOG_USER_PROMPTS behavior — teams already logging prompts will automatically start receiving response content on upgrade unless they set the var to 0.
